review: 小时候玩《战地1942》和《战地2》，打人机模式，第一次感受扮演小兵参与大战场，能够操作各种兵种，驾驶坦克、装甲车、战斗机之类的载具，体验非常新奇，是我美好的童年回忆。虽然一直都是打人机，但是玩得很开心。本FPS苦手打不了PVP，一直想再玩玩战地系列的新作，但是听说都没有单机的人机模式了，只有这代2042还保留了这个模式，上次打折顶着差评还是入手了。今天想体验一下，没想到进游戏都进不去，还得先让玩家进入BIOS开安全启动才能游玩，这么多年没见过这么恶心玩家的。虽然也没有多复杂，但是这个行为太逆天了，不想惯着，花了钱玩个游戏都要被人当孙子，要是老游戏不兼容现在的系统折腾折腾就算了，这21年的游戏搞这套，直接卸载了。

review_english: When I was a child, I played the human-machine mode of Battlefield 1942 and Battlefield 2. It was the first time I felt the role of a soldier participating in a big battlefield. I was able to operate various types of troops and drive tanks, armored vehicles, fighter jets and other vehicles. The experience was very novel and a wonderful childhood memory of mine. Although I have always played human-machine games, I had a lot of fun playing it. This FPS is difficult to play in PVP. I have always wanted to play new games in the Battlefield series, but I heard that there is no single-player human-machine mode. Only this generation of 2042 still retains this mode. I bought it despite the bad reviews last time at a discount. I wanted to try it today. I didn’t expect that I couldn’t even enter the game. I had to let the player enter the BIOS and turn on safe boot before playing. I haven’t seen such disgusting players in so many years. Although it’s not very complicated, this behavior is too unnatural. I don’t want to get used to it. If you spend money to play a game, you will be treated as a grandchild. If the old game is not compatible with the current system, just forget it. This is a 21-year-old game. I will uninstall it directly.

review:
Honestly, I played this when it came out and was disappointed there was no single player campaign at all. I then played it with my brother some but never got hooked on it at the time. I never had any of the bugs people complained about when it came out either. I have now revisited it and it's a ton of fun but conquest is the only mode that seems to be playable, breakthrough takes forever to get filled up so you can play it. The extreme weather is a cool touch, wish BF6 had some of that and the maps from this game !

If you pick this one up on sale for a few bucks it's worth experiencing but don't pay full price because the community seems less active than some of the other older BF titles.

review:
Na minha percepção, hoje quase tudo virou battle royale. E quando não é, muitos jogos ainda tentam apelar para a individualidade — o próprio Battlefield 2042 acabou bebendo um pouco dessa fonte. Parece que muitos jogos modernos passaram a focar mais na individualidade e na expressão pessoal do jogador, vendendo constantemente a ideia de que o jogador precisa ser único, especial ou o centro da experiência.

O legal dos Battlefields antigos, de Battlefield 2 até Battlefield 4, era justamente o oposto. No campo de batalha, você não era “você”; era só mais um. Não importava se você era rico ou pobre, bonito ou feio, gordo ou magro. Nada disso tinha importância ali, porque todos eram apenas soldados. Diferente da vida real, não existiam vantagens sociais invisíveis: todos começavam vulneráveis, e era isso que tornava tudo tão simples e interessante.

Uma das lições mais importantes que aprendi veio justamente jogando Battlefield 4. Assim como na vida, quando você entra em uma partida, inevitavelmente vai encontrar pessoas mais rápidas, mais fortes e muito mais experientes do que você. Mas não é divertido morrer a cada dez segundos sem conseguir fazer nada. 

Então o jogo te ensina outra coisa: observe o mapa, entenda para onde o fluxo da batalha está indo, ajude seus aliados e vá entrando no combate aos poucos, conforme se acostuma com o campo.

E quando não é, muitos jogos ainda tentam apelar para a individualidade o próprio Battlefield 2042 acabou bebendo um pouco dessa fonte. Parece que muitos jogos modernos passaram a focar mais na individualidade e na expressão pessoal do jogador, vendendo constantemente a ideia de que o jogador precisa ser único, especial ou o centro da experiência. O legal dos Battlefields antigos, de Battlefield 2 até Battlefield 4, era justamente o oposto. No campo de batalha, você não era você era só mais um. Não importava se você era rico ou pobre, bonito ou feio, gordo ou magro. Nada disso tinha importância ali, porque todos eram apenas soldados. Diferente da vida real, não existiam vantagens sociais invisíveis todos começavam vulneráveis, e era isso que tornava tudo tão simples e interessante. Uma das lições mais importantes que aprendi veio justamente jogando Battlefield 4. Assim como na vida, quando você entra em uma partida, inevitavelmente vai encontrar pessoas mais rápidas, mais fortes e muito mais experientes do que você. Mas não é divertido morrer a cada dez segundos sem conseguir fazer nada. review_english: In my opinion, today almost everything has become a battle royale. And when it isn't, many games still try to appeal to individuality. Battlefield 2042 itself ended up drinking a little from this source. It seems that many modern games have become more focused on the player's individuality and personal expression, constantly selling the idea that the player needs to be unique, special, or the center of the experience. The cool thing about old Battlefields, from Battlefield 2 to Battlefield 4, was just the opposite. On the battlefield, you weren't you, you were just another person. It didn't matter if you were rich or poor, beautiful or ugly, fat or thin. None of that mattered there, because everyone was just soldiers. Unlike real life, there were no invisible social advantages, everyone started out vulnerable, and that was what made everything so simple and interesting. One of the most important lessons I learned came from playing Battlefield 4. Just like in life, when you enter a match, you will inevitably meet people who are faster, stronger and much more experienced than you. But it's no fun dying every ten seconds without being able to do anything. So the game teaches you something else: observe the map, understand where the flow of the battle is going, help your allies and enter the combat little by little, as you get used to the field. You are not Rambo. You won't take down all the enemies alone, running with a knife without taking damage. You're just another soldier, and understanding this completely changes how you play. When you learn to work together as a team and survive within the chaos, you begin to evolve naturally. And eventually something interesting happens, a newbie starts following you, helps you without realizing it and helps you survive longer. In that moment, you understand that now... You are the veteran. Sorry for the rant lol, now let's get to the facts. Is Battlefield 2042 a good game? Yes - amazing graphics - excellent controller and keyboard gameplay - and lots of fun in multiplayer. But there is an important problem currently: Solo Coop mode with bots practically no longer works on official servers. And even when it worked, the bots depended on EA's online servers, meaning it wasn't a true offline mode. Today, the best alternative is community servers, which are actually very good. The downside is that you won't always find - the map you want to play - the specific mode - or a server with ideal ping. In short, if you like multiplayer, it's worth it, but if you prefer to play alone or against bots, the experience can be frustrating.

review:
Battlefield 2042 had one of the roughest launches in the series, but after a lot of updates, it’s honestly in a much better state now.
At its core, the game still delivers those classic Battlefield moments where absolute chaos happens everywhere at once - tanks exploding, helicopters crashing into buildings, entire squads pushing objectives together, and random nonsense somehow turning into cinematic moments.

The gunplay feels solid, movement is smooth, and large-scale battles can be genuinely fun once everything clicks. Portal mode is also a great addition because it lets players revisit older Battlefield content and create some completely ridiculous custom game modes.

That said, the game still carries a lot of problems that stop it from reaching the same level as older Battlefield titles like Battlefield 3, 4, or 1. The specialist system remains controversial, some maps still feel too open, and the overall atmosphere lacks the gritty military vibe that older games had.

The good:
- Large-scale battles can be incredibly fun
- Gunplay feels satisfying
- Movement and traversal are smooth
- Battlefield chaos is still entertaining
- Portal mode is a great feature
- Vehicle combat can be exciting
- Much improved compared to launch
- Weather events look impressive
- Crossplay support is useful
- Some maps and modes are genuinely fun now

The bad:
- Launch damaged the game’s reputation badly
- Specialist system feels less immersive than classic classes
- Some maps feel too empty and oversized
- Teamplay feels weaker than older Battlefield games
- Bugs and server issues still happen
- Vehicle balance can be frustrating
- Atmosphere lacks the gritty feel of previous games
- Destruction feels downgraded compared to older titles
- UI and menus are awkward
- Still doesn’t fully feel like classic Battlefield

Overall, Battlefield 2042 went from being a disaster to being a decent multiplayer shooter. It’s much more enjoyable now than it was at launch, but even after all the improvements, it still feels like a weaker Battlefield game compared to the series at its peak.

review:
Battlefield 2042 est aujourd’hui un excellent jeu. Malgré un lancement très chaotique à sa sortie, le jeu est désormais beaucoup plus stable et la grande majorité des bugs ont été corrigés. Visuellement, le jeu est très réussi et offre une expérience vraiment immersive.

Le principal point négatif reste malheureusement l’avenir du jeu. Avec l’arrivée de Battlefield 6, il est peu probable que de grosses mises à jour continuent d’être déployées, ce qui est assez dommage pour la communauté encore active.

Malgré cela, le jeu reste très agréable à jouer, surtout entre amis. Les cartes sont excellentes, les véhicules sont variés et amusants à utiliser, et l’ambiance des combats reste très réussie.

Je recommande donc clairement le jeu.

Avec l arrivée de Battlefield 6, il est peu probable que de grosses mises à jour continuent d être déployées, ce qui est assez dommage pour la communauté encore active. Malgré cela, le jeu reste très agréable à jouer, surtout entre amis. Les cartes sont excellentes, les véhicules sont variés et amusants à utiliser, et l ambiance des combats reste très réussie. Je recommande donc clairement le jeu.
review_english: Battlefield 2042 is an excellent game today. Despite a very chaotic launch upon release, the game is now much more stable and the vast majority of bugs have been fixed. Visually, the game is very successful and offers a truly immersive experience. The main negative point unfortunately remains the future of the game. With the arrival of Battlefield 6, it is unlikely that major updates will continue to be deployed, which is quite a shame for the still active community. Despite this, the game remains very enjoyable to play, especially with friends. The maps are excellent, the vehicles are varied and fun to use, and the combat atmosphere remains very successful. So I definitely recommend the game.

review:
Battlefield 2042는
빡세게 경쟁하기보다는,
편하게 즐기기 좋은 전장 느낌의 FPS였다.

요즘 FPS들은 한 판만 해도 진이 빠질 정도로
경쟁 중심인 경우가 많은데,
Battlefield 2042는 아님.

물론 잘하면 좋지만,
이 게임은 순수하게 “전쟁 난장판” 자체를 즐기는 맛이 크다.

탱크 지나가고,
헬기 날아다니고,
건물 무너지고,
사방에서 총알 날아오는 상황 속에서
정신없이 뛰어다니다 보면
이 게임만의 분위기가 있다.

특히 대규모 전투 특유의 스케일이 정말 인상적이었다.

혼자 엄청 캐리해야 한다는 부담보다는,
팀 전체 전장 속 한 명이 되어
자유롭게 플레이하는 느낌에 가깝다.

그래서 가끔은 진지하게 하기보다
친구들이랑 웃으면서 하기 좋다.

갑자기 차량에 치이고,
낙하하다 폭발에 휘말리고,
말도 안 되는 상황에서 살아남는 순간들이
오히려 Battlefield다운 재미로 느껴진다.

그리고 게임 템포도 비교적 자유로운 
편이라 빡센 랭크 게임에 지쳤을 때 하면
은근히 힐링되는 느낌도 있다.

완벽한 게임은 아니지만,
캐주얼하게 큰 전장을 즐기고 싶은 사람들에게는
확실히 매력 있는 FPS라고 생각한다.

review_english: Battlefield 2042 was an FPS with a battlefield feel that was easy to enjoy rather than intense competition. These days, FPSs are often focused on competition to the point where you get exhausted after just one game, but not Battlefield 2042. Of course, it's good if you do well, but this game is purely about enjoying the chaos of war itself. As you run around frantically with tanks passing by, helicopters flying, buildings collapsing, and bullets flying from all directions, there is an atmosphere unique to this game. In particular, the unique scale of large-scale battles was truly impressive. Rather than feeling the pressure of having to carry a lot on your own, it's closer to the feeling of being able to play freely as one of the entire team on the battlefield. So sometimes, it's better to laugh with friends rather than doing it seriously. Moments like suddenly being hit by a car, being caught in an explosion while falling, and surviving in absurd situations actually feel like Battlefield fun. Also, the game tempo is relatively free, so you can feel a sense of healing when you play it when you are tired of the intense ranked game. It's not a perfect game, but I think it's definitely an attractive FPS for people who want to enjoy a large battlefield casually.

review:
Despite the awful launch, EA has, for some reason, decided to stick with this game. In my eyes, it has redeemed itself with the addition of Team Deathmatch (my favorite game mode), the removal of most bugs (I haven't encountered any since I started playing), the addition of new guns, cool cosmetics like Isaac from Dead Space, and the promise of continued support.
However, it lacks a campaign, which is disappointing. The game also features some of the worst maps in the series, with so much open space that shotguns never seem viable. The snipers don’t feel as satisfying as they did in Battlefield 1, though they are marginally better than in Battlefield 4 and Battlefield V, in my opinion. The weapon progression is horrendous, i can't believe i have to play more than 10 hours to even use a revolver. The Specialists aren't really notable.
The best thing about Battlefield 2042 is its frequent big discounts and the chaotic fun it delivers.

The maps feel big and empty with all enemies gathered in a single place. There is some cool environment destruction but not like in the other battlefield games where entire buildings would collapse. To me this game feels more like a competitive shooter rather than a battlefield.

review:
# Análise: Battlefield 2042 (2021)

Esse sofreu um hate absurdo no lançamento. A galera destilou um ódio pesado por ele se passar no futuro e por causa dos problemas técnicos, mas, para mim, é um jogo bom. Eu gosto bastante dele. Ele traz muita coisa que já tinha nos outros Battlefields, tem um gráfico muito bom e uma gameplay excelente. Eu realmente não vejo a necessidade desse ódio todo em cima do jogo. Ele peca em bastante coisa e tem até mais contras do que prós, mas em uma promoção compensa demais. Eu mesmo já peguei ele na faixa dos 50 contos e valeu super a pena.

### 1. O Erro dos Especialistas e a Falta de Campanha

O jogo peca muito feio na questão das classes. Os caras enfiaram um sistema de Especialistas que tirou aquela identidade clássica de esquadrão da franquia e trouxe uma pegada mais parecida com Call of Duty. Isso não combina de jeito nenhum com a proposta de Battlefield, que sempre foi focada em jogo de equipe com funções bem definidas. Para piorar, ele também peca bastante na questão de campanha, já que não tem bosta nenhuma de modo história, sendo um jogo focado 100% no online.

### 2. Destruição Esdrúxula e o Furacão

Outro ponto fraco é o sistema de destruição de cenário. Para um Battlefield, ele é bem esdrúxulo. Pouca coisa de fato vai quebrar nos mapas e tem pouquíssima variação de destruição real em tempo real. Os caras colocaram aquele evento do furacão no meio da partida que, visualmente, até impressiona na primeira vez, mas na prática nem impacta direito a jogabilidade ou a estrutura do mapa. Fica parecendo mais um efeito visual do que uma mecânica de destruição de verdade.

### 3. Guerra em Grande Escala e o Modo com Bots (Golaço)

Apesar dos erros, o jogo é muito bacana quando você quer aquela bagunça de guerra em grande escala. Ele te dá a possibilidade de enfiar uma porrada de gente para jogar no mesmo mapa, com partidas de 64 players (ou até 128 dependendo do modo), o que gera um caos muito divertido.

E um ponto em que os caras acertaram bastante é o modo onde dá para jogar só contra os Bots. Para mim, todo jogo focado em multiplayer devia ter essa opção. É excelente para você treinar a mira, testar os veículos, upar as armas sem estresse ou simplesmente jogar de boa sem a toxicidade do online.

### Conclusão

*Battlefield 2042* passou longe de ser o jogo perfeito e com certeza tomou decisões que desagradaram os fãs mais puristas da saga, mas está longe de ser a porcaria que a internet pintou. A troca de tiros é gostosa e a escala das batalhas diverte bastante. Pelo preço cheio não vale o investimento por causa dos seus defeitos, mas se você achar ele perdido em uma promoção camarada — como na faixa de 50 reais que eu peguei —, compensa demais garantir na Steam para passar o tempo metendo o louco nos mapas gigantes.

review_english: Analysis Battlefield 2042 (2021) This one suffered an absurd amount of hate at launch. People expressed heavy hatred because it takes place in the future and because of the technical problems, but, for me, it's a good game. I like him a lot. It brings a lot of things that already existed in other Battlefields, it has very good graphics and excellent gameplay. I really don't see the need for all this hate towards the game. It lacks a lot of things and even has more cons than pros, but in a promotion it makes up for it a lot. I myself already got it for around 50 contos and it was totally worth it. 1. The Experts' Error and the Lack of Campaign The game has a very bad problem with the class issue. The guys introduced a Specialist system that took away that classic squad identity from the franchise and brought a feel more similar to Call of Duty. This does not at all fit with Battlefield's proposal, which has always been focused on team play with well-defined roles. To make matters worse, it also fails a lot when it comes to the campaign, as there is no story mode at all, being a game focused 100% online. 2. Weird Destruction and the Hurricane Another weak point is the scenario destruction system. For a Battlefield, it's pretty weird. Little will actually break on the maps and there is very little variation in actual destruction in real time. The guys put that hurricane event in the middle of the match which, visually, is impressive the first time, but in practice it doesn't really impact the gameplay or the structure of the map. It looks more like a visual effect than an actual destruction mechanic. 3. Large-Scale War and Bot Mode (Golaço) Despite the errors, the game is very cool when you want that mess of large-scale war. It gives you the possibility of getting a bunch of people to play on the same map, with matches featuring 64 players (or up to 128 depending on the mode), which creates a lot of fun chaos. And one point where the guys got it right is the mode where you can only play against Bots. For me, every game focused on multiplayer should have this option. It's excellent for you to train your aim, test vehicles, upgrade weapons without stress or simply play nice without the toxicity of online. Conclusion Battlefield 2042 was far from being the perfect game and certainly made decisions that displeased the most purist fans of the saga, but it is far from being the rubbish that the internet painted it to be. The exchange of fire is enjoyable and the scale of the battles is a lot of fun. At full price it's not worth the investment because of its flaws, but if you find it lost in a friendly promotion like the 50 reais range I got, it's worth it to secure it on Steam to spend time getting crazy on the giant maps.

review:
Madre mía... Qué decir de este juego...
Para empezar, le pasa como a los demás battlefield: Tienes que tener el launcher propio de EA que directamente es bazofia. Dejemos de lado la crisis de identidad que tiene este "battlefield" entre seguir teniendo "destrucción" y parecer un dlc del modo guerra terrestre de COD MW 2019 con reescalado de gráficos para un pc patata. No solamente es el battlefield con menos personalidad y más feo gráficamente comparado con los demás que sacaron estos últimos años, sino que encima pide requisitos altísimos y está lleno de bugs. Ya no hablemos del tema de quitar el chat y poner el sistema de operadores con menos salero que he visto en mi vida. 
Y bueno, lo mejor de todo: el matchmaking funciona peor que un Seat León amarillo del 2005 con 350 mil km al que no se le ha cambiado el aceite en la vida. Tienes que hacer malabares para que te funcione: Hay un error reportado por varios usuarios (entre ellos yo) en el que encuentra partida y directamente se ponía la pantalla en negro y te devolvía al lobby. Así hasta que mágicamente se quiso arreglar y me metió a una partida que parecía que estaba jugando desde un servidor de la extinta Yugoslavia. 
"Oye pero tendrías mala conexión a internet, no?"
20 de ping, señores.

review: After playing Battlefield 2042, I can say the game delivers some really intense and cinematic multiplayer moments. The large-scale battles, explosions, vehicles, and weather effects make matches feel chaotic and exciting, especially when everything is happening at once. The graphics and sound design are impressive, and the modern setting gives access to fun weapons, gadgets, and vehicles. Some maps can feel too large at times, and the game had a rough launch with bugs and performance problems, which many players still remember. However, after updates, the experience feels much better and more enjoyable than before. Overall, Battlefield 2042 may not be the strongest Battlefield game for longtime fans, but it still offers fast-paced action and fun multiplayer moments for players who enjoy large-scale warfare games.

review:
Man stelle sich folgendes Szenario vor: 
Eine Person fährt zu einem Amt, zieht eine Nummer und setzt sich in den Wartebereich. Dort wartet diese bis zum Sankt-Nimmerleins-Tag und erreicht dennoch nicht ihr Ziel, da sie aus unerklärlichen Gründen nicht drangenommen wird. Nicht nur hat die besagte Person überhaupt nichts erreicht, sondern zusätzlich noch Geld und Zeit verloren.
So fühlt sich Battlefield 2042 an.

Nachdem man Geld ausgegeben, einen Berg an Speicherplatz für ein einziges Spiel geopfert und sich lange darauf gefreut hat, ist das Resultat alles andere als zufriedenstellend. Trotz äußerst stabiler Internetverbindung und einem leistungsstarken PC sind die Lade- und Wartezeiten so bodenlos, dass man in der Zeit die Welt mit einem Paddelboot umrunden könnte. Hinzu kommt noch, dass TDM der einzige Modus ist, welcher mit Cross Play funktioniert. Für die anderen Spielmodi muss man diese Funktion deaktivieren, damit man nach minutenlangem Warten und einem plötzlichen Blackscreen nicht in das Hauptmenü zurückgeworfen wird. Da fragt man sich: Warum wird Cross Play dann überhaupt hier als Option angeboten? Das ist so sinnvoll, wie eine Pizza mit Stäbchen zu Essen.
Wenn ich ein Baby an das Steuer meines PKWs setzen und diesem befehlen würde mich von Hamburg nach Berlin zu fahren, würde es sich wahrscheinlich so fühlen wie unerfahrene Spieler hier, in Battlefield 2042. Ich habe bereits einige Erfahrung mit Shootern, weshalb ich die Steuerung intuitiv relativ leicht verstand, aber Spieler mit keiner oder wenig Erfahrung, welche einfach ins kalte Wasser geworfen werden und keinerlei Erklärung über die Steuerung, Spielmechaniken, Items etc. bekommen, sind hier wirklich verloren. Von Tutorials oder Hilfestellungen ist nicht wirklich eine Spur zu finden.
Das Gameplay an sich ist in Ordnung, auch wenn dieses gewisse Mängel und Eintönigkeiten aufweist, was ich nicht weiter ausführen möchte, aber das viele Geld ist es definitiv nicht wert. Da machen die Konkurrenten einen deutlich besseren Job.

Mein persönliches Fazit:
Nicht mal einen durchgeweichten Butterkeks würde ich hierfür eintauschen, da man mit diesem noch deutlich mehr Freude hätte.

review_english: Imagine the following scenario: A person drives to an office, takes a number and sits in the waiting area. It waits there until St. Nicholas Day and still doesn't reach its destination because for some inexplicable reason it isn't accepted. Not only did the person in question achieve nothing, but they also lost money and time. This is what Battlefield 2042 feels like. After spending money, sacrificing a mountain of storage space for a single game, and looking forward to it for a long time, the result is anything but satisfactory. Despite an extremely stable internet connection and a powerful PC, the loading and waiting times are so incredible that you could circumnavigate the world in a paddle boat in that time. In addition, TDM is the only mode that works with cross play. For the other game modes you have to deactivate this function so that you are not thrown back into the main menu after waiting for minutes and a sudden black screen. This makes you wonder why is cross play even offered as an option here? This is as useful as eating pizza with chopsticks. If I put a baby behind the wheel of my car and told it to drive me from Hamburg to Berlin, it would probably feel the same way as inexperienced players do here, in Battlefield 2042. I already have some experience with shooters, which is why I intuitively understood the controls relatively easily, but players with little or no experience who are simply thrown into the deep end and receive no explanation about the controls, game mechanics, items, etc. are really lost here. There isn't really any trace of tutorials or assistance to be found. The gameplay itself is fine, even if it has certain flaws and monotony, which I don't want to go into, but it's definitely not worth the money. The competitors are doing a much better job. My personal conclusion I wouldn't even swap a soggy shortbread for this, as it would be much more fun with it.

review:
Battlefield 2042 arrived with enormous expectations. Developed by DICE and published by Electronic Arts, the game promised massive warfare, dynamic weather events, and the next evolution of the long-running Battlefield formula. What players received at launch, however, was one of the franchise’s most divisive entries.

At its best, Battlefield 2042 captures the cinematic chaos the series is famous for. Massive 128-player battles create moments that few shooters can replicate. Tanks roll across deserts while helicopters swarm overhead and tornadoes tear through the battlefield, creating scenes that feel genuinely spectacular. The scale is undeniably impressive.

Gunplay feels responsive in many situations, and the Plus System, which allows players to swap attachments on weapons in real time, is one of the game’s strongest innovations. It encourages adaptability during combat and keeps firefights dynamic. Vehicle call-ins and large-scale sandbox gameplay also reinforce the franchise’s trademark freedom.

The standout feature is arguably Battlefield Portal. This mode acts as a celebration of the franchise’s history, bringing back content from older titles like Battlefield 3 and Bad Company 2. For longtime fans, Portal often feels more authentic to classic Battlefield than the core game itself.

Unfortunately, the game’s problems are difficult to ignore. At launch, Battlefield 2042 suffered from major technical issues, unstable servers, bugs, missing features, and controversial design decisions. Critics and players heavily targeted the oversized maps, lack of class-based gameplay, and weak team coordination systems.

The new Specialist system became one of the game’s most criticized elements. Traditional Battlefield classes were replaced with hero-style characters, pushing the series closer to modern hero shooters. Many fans felt this change damaged the teamwork-focused identity that defined earlier entries.

Community reception reflected those frustrations. Steam reviews dropped to extremely low approval ratings during launch, and many longtime fans described the release as unfinished.

To DICE’s credit, the game improved significantly through updates. Maps were redesigned, gameplay systems were adjusted, and overall stability became far better than it was in 2021. Many returning players now describe the experience as enjoyable, even if it never fully reaches the heights of older Battlefield titles.

Still, Battlefield 2042 feels like a game caught between identities. It wants to modernize the franchise while preserving the sandbox warfare fans expect, but the balance never completely settles. Moments of brilliance appear regularly, yet they are often surrounded by design choices that dilute the classic Battlefield experience.

Today, Battlefield 2042 is far better than its launch version and can provide exciting large-scale multiplayer action, especially with friends. But it also remains a reminder that ambition alone cannot replace strong direction and polish.

Score: 7.2/10

review:
【总评：适合独狼/PVE玩家的杀时间利器，但细节仍有遗憾】
​作为一名主要冲着**单人模式（全面战争/突破模式打AI）**入手的玩家，体验有些话想说：
​➕ 优点：
​PVE玩家的福音： 这次最让我满意的就是完整的单人/合作模式。不像《战地1》和《战地5》打完单人战役就只能去多人联机，2042能让我安安静静地和AI打完整场比赛，不需要考虑那么多复杂的社交或竞技压力，纯粹就是享受游戏、放松解压。
​载具与枪械： 游戏里的枪械手感和载具驾驶我个人挺喜欢的，开着载具或者拿着喜欢的枪刷AI，爽感很足。
​➖ 缺点/遗憾：
​单人刷级有上限： 单人模式下的武器和载具挂件刷到**大师12（T12）**之后就到头了，后续的进度和皮肤去多人联机 或者特定门户才能解锁。
​专家系统有些冗余： 感觉专家分得有点太多、太杂了，对于单人体验来说，传统的兵种划分可能更干净利落。
​地图补给设计问题： 地图上没有固定的弹药补给点， 对空和打载具很难受 。如果不自己带弹药箱或者选特定专家，续航成问题，这点对单人游玩不太友好。
飞机的手感有点怪很重
​总结：
如果你和我一样，买战地只是想在下班/ 想玩游戏解压开一局单人模式、开着载具打打AI享受游戏，那它绝对能满足你。 皮肤 枪和载具不同类型有个一两个就差不多了 不需要全部是

review_english: Overall review: A time-killing tool suitable for lone wolf PVE players, but there are still regrets in the details. As a player who mainly focuses on single-player mode, Total War, and Breakthrough mode to fight AI, I have something to say about the experience. Advantages Good news for PVE players. What makes me most satisfied this time is the complete single-player cooperative mode. Unlike Battlefield 1 and Battlefield 5, I can only go to multiplayer after finishing the single-player campaign. 2042 allows me to play the entire game quietly with the AI. There is no need to consider so many complicated social or competitive pressures. Just enjoy the game and relax and decompress. Vehicles and firearms. I personally like the feel of firearms and vehicle driving in the game. It feels very satisfying to drive a vehicle or use your favorite gun to master the AI. Disadvantages: Unfortunately, there is an upper limit for single-player leveling. Weapons and vehicle attachments in single-player mode are reached after master 12 and T12. It is over. Subsequent progress and skins can only be unlocked through multiplayer or specific portals. The expert system is somewhat redundant. I feel that experts are divided too much and too complicated. For a single-player experience, the traditional division of arms may be cleaner. Map supply design issues. There are no fixed ammunition supply points on the map. It is difficult to fight against air and vehicles. If you do not bring your own ammunition boxes or choose specific experts, battery life is a problem. This is not very friendly to single-player play. The aircraft feels a bit strange and heavy. Summary: If you are like me, you only want to buy Battlefield just to get off work and want to play games to decompress and start a single-player mode. If you enjoy the game by driving a vehicle and beating the AI, then it will definitely satisfy you. One or two of different types of skins, guns and vehicles are enough. You don’t need to use all of them.

review:
[h2] Una redención a medias, pero divertido con amigos [/h2]

Después de haberle dedicado unas cuantas horas y probar su estado actual, mi experiencia con [b]Battlefield™ 2042[/b] es una mezcla de luces y sombras. El juego ha cambiado mucho desde su caótico lanzamiento, pero sigue teniendo decisiones de diseño que no terminan de convencer a los fans de la vieja escuela.

[h3] Lo que me ha gustado: [/h3]
[list]
[] [b]El modo Portal:[/b] Es, sin duda, lo mejor del juego. Poder revivir mapas, armas y clases de entregas clásicas como [i]Bad Company 2[/i] o [i]Battlefield 3[/i] es una delicia nostálgica.
[] [b]Rendimiento mejorado:[/b] El juego ya no es el festival de bugs y optimización desastrosa que era al principio. Hoy en día se juega de forma bastante fluida.
[*] [b]El gunplay y la acción:[/b] Cuando te metes de lleno en una partida de Conquista con 128 jugadores y el caos estalla, se logran momentos muy "Battlefield".
[/list]

[h3] Lo que NO me ha gustado: [/h3]
[list]
[] [b]El sistema de Especialistas:[/b] Sustituir las clases tradicionales por "héroes" con nombre y apellido le quitó gran parte de la identidad militar y el juego en equipo orgánico de la saga. Aunque lo han parcheado para imitar el sistema antiguo, no es lo mismo.
[] [b]Diseño de mapas originales:[/b] Muchos de los mapas base se sienten demasiado grandes, vacíos y planos, lo que te obliga a correr durante minutos por campo abierto solo para que te elimine un vehículo.
[*] [b]Falta de alma de guerra:[/b] Se extraña la atmósfera oscura, destructiva e inmersiva que tenían [i]Battlefield 1[/i] o [i]Battlefield V[/i].
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h2] ¿Lo recomendaría? [/h2]

[b]Sí, pero solo en oferta.[/b] Si lo encuentras a un precio muy rebajado o juegas con un grupo de amigos para echaros unas risas en el caos de 128 jugadores, vale la pena darle una oportunidad en su estado actual. Si buscas la experiencia táctica y militar seria de los antiguos Battlefield, es posible que te deje un sabor de boca agridulce.

[b]Nota personal:[/b] 6.5/10

review_english: h2 A half-hearted redemption, but fun with friends h2 After spending a few hours and testing its current state, my experience with b Battlefield 2042 b is a mix of lights and shadows. The game has changed a lot since its chaotic launch, but it still has design decisions that don't convince old-school fans. h3 What I liked h3 list b Portal mode b It is, without a doubt, the best thing about the game. Being able to relive maps, weapons and classes from classic installments like i Bad Company 2 i or i Battlefield 3 i is a nostalgic delight. b Improved performance b The game is no longer the bug-fest and disastrous optimization it was at the beginning. Nowadays it plays quite smoothly. b Gunplay and action b When you get into a Conquest game with 128 players and chaos breaks out, you get very "Battlefield" moments. list h3 What I did NOT like h3 list b The Specialist system b Replacing traditional classes with "heroes" with a first and last name took away much of the military identity and organic team play of the saga. Although they have patched it to mimic the old system, it is not the same. b Original map design b Many of the basemaps feel overly large, empty and flat, forcing you to run for minutes through open countryside only to be taken out by a vehicle. b Lack of soul of war b The dark, destructive and immersive atmosphere they had i Battlefield 1 i or i Battlefield V i is missed. list hr hr h2 Would you recommend it? h2 b Yes, but only on sale. b If you find it at a very discounted price or play with a group of friends to have a few laughs in the chaos of 128 players, it is worth giving it a chance in its current state. If you're looking for the serious tactical and military experience of the old Battlefield games, it may leave a bittersweet taste in your mouth. b Personal note b 6.5 10

Из всех 35 минут что я провел в игре - играл я только первые 10-15 минут в обучение по засыпанному городу. Не увидел ничего классного в этой игре, при запуске в катку выкидывает в лобби. И так с десяток раз)

Гуглил проблему, люди массово жалуются, что в катку не залетает, и были идеи, что это из-за BF6. Типа разрабы "ломают" BF2042 ради онлайна в BF6. Зато трясут за эту помойку 20к тенге в обычном издании и 30к в элитном, где добавляются пару скинов...

Благо, я урвал эту игру со скидкой 95% и она обошлась мне в сущие копейки. Не представляю, как бы я бомбил, если бы купил её за 7к рублей.

В общем, тем, кто её купил - сочувствую, а кто ещё нет - КРАЙНЕ не рекомендую к покупке.

Типа разрабы "ломают" BF2042 ради онлайна в BF6. Зато трясут за эту помойку 20к тенге в обычном издании и 30к в элитном, где добавляются пару скинов... Благо, я урвал эту игру со скидкой 95 и она обошлась мне в сущие копейки. Не представляю, как бы я бомбил, если бы купил её за 7к рублей. В общем, тем, кто её купил - сочувствую, а кто ещё нет - КРАЙНЕ не рекомендую к покупке. Подытоживая, за 0.6ч игры я так и не поиграл в игру.
review_english: Of all the 35 minutes that I spent in the game, I only played the first 10-15 minutes of training in the covered city. I didn’t see anything cool in this game; when I launch it, it throws me into the lobby. And so a dozen times) I Googled the problem, people were complaining en masse that it wouldn’t fly into the skating rink, and there were ideas that it was because of BF6. Like the developers are “breaking” BF2042 for the sake of online in BF6. But they charge 20k tenge for this garbage in the regular edition and 30k in the elite edition, where a couple of skins are added... Fortunately, I snatched this game with a 95 discount and it cost me mere pennies. I can’t imagine how much I would bomb if I bought it for 7k rubles. In general, I sympathize with those who bought it, but I HIGHLY do not recommend buying it for those who haven’t yet. To summarize, after 0.6 hours of play, I still didn’t play the game.

review:
[h3] Не стоит вашего времени и 300 рублей по скидке [/h3]

К самой игре у меня нет вопросов. Перед покупкой посмотрел много свежих обзоров, плюс сам поиграл какое-то время. С момента релиза игра прошла долгий путь, чтобы стать нормальным мультиплеерным шутером, а не тем недоразумением, что была на старте. Не шедевром, но крепким середничком.

И всё бы ничего, вот только... В игру невозможно поиграть стабильно. Сервер находит, но коннектит к нему раз через раз. Порой после одной игры приходилось 15 минут пытаться запустить хоть какой-либо режим. Рано или поздно терпение заканчивалось, и игра просто закрывалась.

Не помогал и VPN. Разные VPN. На форумах по игре десятки аналогичных жалоб. Кого благодарить за это, я не знаю: то ли забугорный EA, то ли наш родной [spoiler]РКН[/spoiler].

В любом случае, вы больше намучаетесь с подбором, чем получите удовольствие от тех немногочисленных игр, что посчастливится сыграть. Лучше сходите в Delta Force или бесплатные режимы BF6. Такая история.

P. S. Ладно, вопрос к игре всё же есть. Раздражает дисбаланс техники против пехоты. 

Для уничтожения среднебронированного транспорта нужно 4 выстрела из РПГ, в то время как у тебя всего 3 снаряда. Рассчитывать на присутствие рядом тиммейта, который поможет скинуться по технике, в 9 из 10 случаев не приходится.

Но и это ещё не всё. После первого же выстрела игра подсвечивает тебя для врагов, из-за чего ты становишься лёгкой мишенью и практически гарантированно погибаешь, не успев дострелять свои несчастные 3 снаряда.

А кайфорез в транспорте давит педаль газа дальше как ни в чём не бывало (его ещё и подлатает кто-нибудь до твоего редиплоя). Чеснок?

review_english: h3 Not worth your time and 300 rubles at a discount h3 I have no questions about the game itself. Before purchasing, I looked at a lot of recent reviews, plus I played for a while. Since its release, the game has come a long way to become a normal multiplayer shooter, and not the misunderstanding that it was at the start. Not a masterpiece, but a strong middling one. And everything would be fine, but... It’s impossible to play the game consistently. The server finds it, but connects to it every other time. Sometimes after one game I had to spend 15 minutes trying to launch any mode. Sooner or later, patience ran out and the game simply closed. VPN didn't help either. Different VPNs. There are dozens of similar complaints on the game forums. I don’t know who to thank for this, either the foreign EA, or our native spoiler RKN spoiler. In any case, you will suffer more from the selection than you will enjoy the few games that you are lucky enough to play. Better go to Delta Force or free modes of BF6. Such a story. P.S. Okay, there is still a question about the game. The imbalance of vehicles versus infantry is annoying. To destroy a medium armored vehicle you need 4 shots from an RPG, while you only have 3 shells. In 9 out of 10 cases, you can’t count on the presence of a teammate nearby who will help you chip in on technology. But that's not all. After the first shot, the game highlights you for enemies, which is why you become an easy target and are almost guaranteed to die before you have time to finish shooting your unfortunate 3 shells. And the buzz in transport presses the gas pedal further as if nothing had happened (someone will also patch it up before your rediploy). Garlic?

Плюсы:
Бодрый онлайн и хорошая стрельба (сейчас играется гораздо лучше, чем на релизе).
Большие масштабы, много экшена.
Минусы:
Игра ощущается «пластиковой» и слишком стерильной по сравнению с прошлыми частями.
Потеряна та самая физика, атмосфера и «тяжесть» войны из прошлых BF.
Итог: Хорошая игра для залипания в мультиплеере, но слабая Battlefield. Рекомендую с оговорками.

review_english: Pros: Lively online and good shooting (plays much better now than at release). Big scale, lots of action. Cons The game feels plastic and too sterile compared to previous games. The same physics, atmosphere and severity of war from past BFs are lost. Summary A good game for getting stuck in multiplayer, but weak Battlefield. I recommend it with reservations.

Because there are always people you can’t beat, it can easily affect your mood. It’s just a game. Happiness is the most important thing, right? Secondly, although there are not many guns in it, many of them are close to reality. I checked a few, and they are all real. Third, regarding the collection of personal information online, I think there are not many companies that don’t collect your information. review:
Не понимаю, откуда столько критики к Battlefield 2042. Вы поймите, что бедной инди-студии EA тяжело довести игру до рабочего состояния за 5 лет с даты выхода. Это вам не многомиллионный блокбастер - пара энтузиастов в гараже, без сна и еды, буквально из последних сил пытаются склеить строчки кода.

Особого восхищения заслуживает их свежайшая инновация, введенная в последнем обновлении, - режим «Чёрный экран и вылет в главное меню». Гениально! Тебя не просто выкидывает из матча, а проводит через эмоциональные качели: секунды саспенса перед абсолютной чернотой, замирание сердца и… бам - резкое возвращение в лобби без упоминания какой либо ошибки, будто так и задумано. Зачем какая-то стрельба и разрушаемость, если можно бесконечно медитировать на этот чёрный квадрат Малевича? Стабильность работы впечатляет - я ещё ни разу случайно не попал в бой. Браво, DICE, вы превзошли сами себя. Жду теперь DLC «Оплати чёрный экран, чтобы увидеть чёрный экран». 10/10 на кончиках пальцев.
review_clean: Не понимаю, откуда столько критики к Battlefield 2042. Вы поймите, что бедной инди-студии EA тяжело довести игру до рабочего состояния за 5 лет с даты выхода. Это вам не многомиллионный блокбастер - пара энтузиастов в гараже, без сна и еды, буквально из последних сил пытаются склеить строчки кода. Особого восхищения заслуживает их свежайшая инновация, введенная в последнем обновлении, - режим Чёрный экран и вылет в главное меню . Гениально! Тебя не просто выкидывает из матча, а проводит через эмоциональные качели секунды саспенса перед абсолютной чернотой, замирание сердца и бам - резкое возвращение в лобби без упоминания какой либо ошибки, будто так и задумано. Зачем какая-то стрельба и разрушаемость, если можно бесконечно медитировать на этот чёрный квадрат Малевича? Стабильность работы впечатляет - я ещё ни разу случайно не попал в бой. Браво, DICE, вы превзошли сами себя. Жду теперь DLC Оплати чёрный экран, чтобы увидеть чёрный экран . 10 10 на кончиках пальцев.
review_english: I don’t understand why there is so much criticism of Battlefield 2042. You understand that it is difficult for the poor indie studio EA to bring the game to a working state in 5 years from the release date. This is not a multimillion-dollar blockbuster - a couple of enthusiasts in a garage, without sleep or food, literally trying with all their might to glue together lines of code. Their latest innovation, introduced in the latest update, deserves special admiration - Black screen mode and flight to the main menu. Brilliant! You are not just thrown out of the match, but put through an emotional swing of seconds of suspense before absolute blackness, a sinking heart and bam - a sharp return to the lobby without mention of any mistake, as if it was intended to be so. Why any shooting and destructibility if you can endlessly meditate on this black square of Malevich? The stability of operation is impressive - I have never accidentally gotten into battle yet. Bravo, DICE, you have outdone yourself. Now I'm waiting for the DLC Pay for a black screen to see a black screen. 10 10 at your fingertips.
